subject: Cloud Computing
title: Accidental Public Exposure Costs
description: A short lesson to help you apply Accidental Public Exposure Costs.

hook.text: A misconfigured S3 bucket serves files publicly. Bots discover it and download terabytes. The bill explodes.

buildup.text: Public-facing cloud resources can be exploited by anyone. Bots, scrapers, and attackers generate massive traffic.

discovery.text: Some victims have received bills in the hundreds of thousands from resources they didn't know were public.

twist.text: AWS has started waiving some of these charges. But the policy varies — and there's no guarantee of forgiveness.

climax.text: Prevention is key: block public access by default, monitor for unusual traffic spikes, and set hard budget limits.

punchline.text: One public bucket. Millions in downloads. A shocking bill.

quiz.question: How can accidental public exposure lead to unexpected cloud bills?
options: Bots and scrapers download massive amounts of data, generating huge egress charges
Public resources automatically upgrade to premium pricing
Cloud providers charge a fine for every publicly exposed resource
